This is an educational children's game intended to teach them fractions.  Players compete to correctly identify fractions and fraction calculations while avoiding the "Green Goblin."  Game includes a variety of cards with fractions and partial fractions, pie charts, and a green goblin.

The object of B.S. Degree Game is to earn enough college credits (units) to graduate before any other players.

The board is laid out into four quadrants; Freshman, Sophomore, Junior and Senior years.

The playing time is approximately 1-2 hours for two players. Add additional 30-60 minutes for each additional player.

There are also instructions for using other trivia games in conjunction with this one.

Players put words to definitions to advance.  Go from home to the library and back.  Three different types of cards: Amateur, Intermediate, Expert.  Several different definitions per word.  5 playing pieces are provided, and it is suggested that for more players people organize into groups.

At the beginning of the round, 4 cards with different numbers are revealed in a 2x2 pattern.
Each player has five two-faced cards with 4 numbers on each of them, and must use 4 of them to create, as quickly as possible, a 3x3 grid where each number is present once (just like in Sudoku) and the 2x2 pattern of numbers is present as well.
The quickest player wins the round - the winner of the game is the first player to win 3 rounds.

In "Malhação - O Jogo", players are challenged to answer correctly questions about... the other players! Each turn, a player reads out loud a card with a question, and secretly choses one out of three options about how he (or she) would react in such situation. After that, it's time for the others to try to guess which was the chosen answer.

The game always have two winners - each player has a separate score with every other player. In the end, the pair that has the highest sum of points wins the game.

This is a version of the game Monopoly based on the theme of Lionel trains (postwar era).  The game has custom money based on vintage Lionel Stock Certificates, Crossing Gate and Banjo Signal Cards, and 6 Collectible Pewter Engine Tokens.
This is a version of Monopoly based on the restaurant chain of Hard Rock Cafes.  The game includes  6 Custom Pewter Tokens (Hurricane Glass, Motorcycle, Hamburger, Guitar, Guitar Pick, and Saxaphone)

This is a version of Monopoly based on the United States Coast Guard.  Players can buy, sell and trade the U.S. Coast Guard’s most prized assets including Diamond Head Lighthouse, USCGC Juniper, and the HH-65 Dolphin in an effort to protect U.S. Homeland Security. Includes six collectible pewter tokens (Eagle, Life ring, Anchor, Cutter, Helicopter, and Lighthouse).
This is a version of Monopoly themed on the 75th Anniversary of the Estes Express Lines trucking company.

This is a version of Monopoly themed on the National Football League (NFL) team the Chicago Bears.  Players can buy and sell many of the team's most famous players, including Walter Payton and Brian Urlacher.

Includes six collectible pewter tokens

This is a version of Monopoly based on the National Football League (NFL) team, the Philadelphia Eagles.  Players can buy and trade the team's most famous players, including Ron Jaworski and Donovan McNabb.

This is a version of the Monopoly game based on the theme of vacation resorts, including the Sunterra resort.  Players can buy, own, and trade some well known resort properties.  Traditional houses and hotels are renamed Units and Resorts, and customized Sunterra themed SunOptions and Exchanges cards replace Chance and Community Chest cards.  Includes 6 custom Pewter Tokens which include: Sunterra Flip Flops, Beach Chair, Golf Cart, Beach Cruiser, Jet, and Ski goggles.

This is a version of Monopoly based on Major League Baseball Players.  This version has a unique mechanism whereby the traditional property spaces start out blank and are filled with baseball players (over 100 players to choose from) as the game progresses and the game players "draft" their fantasy baseball team.  The game includes 6 custom pewter pieces, including: the hot dog, the World Series Trophy, pitcher, batter, ball in glove and catcher’s mask.

boardgame: Monopoly: St. Louis Rams Champions Edition
description: This is a version of Monopoly based on the National Football League (NFL) team the St. Louis Rams, and their championship season in 2000.  The game was limited in production and each copy is individually numbered
This is a Monopoly style game based on the Red Deer Region of Alberta Canada.  The game was produced in conjunction with Habitat for Humanity and its proceeds benefitted that organization.

boardgame: Monopoly: New York Jets Collector's Edition
description: This is a version of the Monopoly game based on the National Footbal League (NFL) team, the New York Jets.  Players can Buy, sell and trade the team's most valuable players, from Joe Namath and Mark Gastineau to Chad Pennington and Curtis Martin.

(Taken from website)

Little Billy got an Ant Ranch for his birthday and sent away for entirely too many ants. You lead one of the unfortunate colonies trying to survive in your new plastic home. To win, you must battle the other ant colonies and survive the many perils, from handing responsibility of the ant ranch over to little brother Johnny (who loves playing with a magnifying glass!), to game's end, when Billy's mom gets fed up with the whole ordeal and throws everything in the trash. Whoever has the most ants when this occurs wins the game!

During your turn, you lay tiles to build the tunnel system your colony thrives in, or play special cards to harvest food and water so you can multiply faster than your opponents. Roll the action-packed aphid die and see what your outcome will be! 2-4 players can play Ant Ranch in 2-3 hours.

Welcome to Rome, circa 510BC. Following generations of tyrannical monarchy, the fledgling republic of Rome has become a political battleground for a few old and powerful families, each now vying for control and influence. As the city grows so do familial ambitions.

Change is inevitable and the Republic will soon fall, only to be replaced by an empire the likes of which the world has never seen... but a great empire needs an emperor of great conceit and wiles. With the rules, alliances, and strategies ever changing, it might not be who you think.

The Republic is a game of political intrigue and city building for three to six players. Although tile placement and economics are important considerations, the true focus of the game is on the politics. Each round, players are required to vote on laws that drastically alter the way the game is played, often affecting players in radically different ways. The winner of the game is usually the first player to collect a set amount of Imperium, a resource which can be earned through well-planned construction or lawmaking.

Adversaries was GDW's series of additional detailed game components (miniatures really) for the many titles under the Space:1889 Banner.

				
				
					Martian Cloudships (GDW 1801)A set of miniature plastic Martian cloudships. The box contains several additional sprues of the Martian Cloudships, identical to the single sprue contained in Sky Galleons of Mars. The true genius here was including kit bashing instructions so that avid players could convert these stock miniatures into other cloudships detailed in yet other Space: 1889 (Original edition) products.

Expands: Sky Galleons of Mars and Ironclads and Ether Flyers

				
				
					Aerial Gunboats (GDW 1802)A similar set of plastic British aerial gunboats. In this case, additional sprues of the British Gunboats from Sky Galleons of Mars with kit bashing instructions to create other gunboats detailed in Space: 1889 (Original edition).

Expands: Sky Galleons of Mars and Ironclads and Ether Flyers

				
				
					Victorian Adventurers (GDW 1821)A set of ten 25mm figures created by RAFM to replace the pawns in Temple of the Beastmen.

From the publisher:

Intrepid Victorian adventurers penetrate the inner sanctum of a High Martian kraag, the mountain fortresses cut from the living stone of tall mesas by the savage winged High Martians.  Each adventurer strives to be the first to fight his way through to the heart of the kraag and discover the sinister Worm Priests.

Expands: Temple of the Beastmen, Soldier's Companion, and Cloudships & Gunboats

				
				
					Kraag Warriors (GDW 1843)A set of twenty 25mm figures (in 10 poses) created by RAFM for use with Temple of the Beastmen and the related role-playing game.

Expands: Temple of the Beastmen, Soldier's Companion, and Cloudships & Gunboats

				
				
					Soldiers of the Queen (GDW 1841)A set of eighteen 25mm figures (in 10 poses) created by RAFM for use with Soldier's Companion and the related role-playing game.

Expands: Soldier's Companion and Cloudships & Gunboats

				
				
					Legions of Mars (GDW 1842)A set of twenty 25mm figures (in 10 poses) created by RAFM for use with Soldier's Companion and the related role-playing game.

Expands: Soldier's Companion and Cloudships & Gunboats
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Versions & Editions: Board Game Versions of Role-Playing Games

boardgame: Babylon 5: A Call to Arms (Second Edition) – Fleet Lists
description:
Accompanying the brand new second edition of the award-winning A Call to Arms Babylon 5 space combat miniatures game, the Babylon 5 A Call to Arms 2nd Ed. Fleet Book contains the comprehensive data necessary to play any of the major and iconic racial fleets of the Babylon 5 universe. The Earth Alliance, Minbari Federation, Centauri Republic, Narn Regime and League of Non-Aligned Worlds have all their ship types and fleets described, along with their game statistics. Also included are fleet lists for the ancient Shadows and Vorlons, as well as expanded information on the fleets of specific League races, such as the Brakiri, the Vree, the Abbai, an other races like the Dilgar, Drakh and multi-racial Raiders.

Finally, there are brand new fleet lists added to the game with this book - the Gaim, pak'mar'ra and Psi Corps!

This is not a complete game and requires the Babylon 5: A Call to Arms – Second Edition

A reimplementation of A Call to Arms: Babylon 5 Space Combat

From the manufacturer:

Ok girls, you have been posting cool truths and dares to the Girl Tech website and now we have created the ultimate truth or dare game for you! Just spin to play and watch the cool lights display your next move, whether it is a Give, Take, Truth, Dare or Double Dare. And guess what, now you can record your very own double dares - you can make them as silly, gross or daring as you want! The Truth Detector let's you test your friends truthfulness. Collect a chip from each category to win the game!

* The ultimate Truth or Dare game
    * Record four double dares of your own
    * Truth Detector
    * 500 Truth or Dare cards

Ages 8 and up

Postcards from America is a unique game where the players learn geography by traveling to cities and other locations based on actual postcards. The game used 64 postcards from the game designers personal travels.  Designed for Ages 7 and up

Each player starts the game with 50,000 cash and pawns are placed on the start. Players then travel around the board (1 trip around equals 1 year) and attempts to collect CD's, Blue Star, Gold Mine, Oil Well, Real Estate, Mutual Fund cards in order to amass Bonus cards.

The first player to collect 5 Bonus cards wins the game.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
The object of the game is to be the first player to attain the Admiral rank.

You start the game as a Midshipman (Naval Cadet) and get promoted to the next higher rank when you achieve the required points at the "Promotion Board".

Use a combination of "Naval Academy" cards and dice roll to help you meet the necessary points. Other players can attempt to sabotage your promotion by using "Top Secret" cards. However, these "Top Secret" cards, once used, are removed from the game, so the timing and frequency of use can be a factor.

ADMIRAL can be classified as a family game - providing enough element of luck for children to win yet offering light strategy play for adults to stay engaged.

From Publisher's Website*


Spinespur is a world where evil reigns supreme and even the angels have stains on their wings. Spinespur is filled with the typical chainsaws, zombies, and werewolves as well as more obscure or innovative nightmares all its own. It’s a Modern Survival Horror game with elements of fantasy alongside elements of science fiction.

Kampen om Örebro is about the Swedish city Orebro. It is a game similar to monopoly with some extra rules such as questions about local company in örebro.

Kampen om Örebro belongs to a series of games called kunskapsspelet.

boardgame: Bicycle Moto Cross Challenge Action Game
description:
"Cross Challenge" is unique.  It will teach you HOW to actually race at any BMX track.

This game is designed for two, three, or four players and it never plays the same way twice.  The competition is identical to racing in a real BMX moto.  "Cross Challenge" is truly a game of skill, but, like any sport, luck also plays a part.  Choose the command card you think will gain you the best position on the board/track and then experience true BMS racing excitement known as "Berm Warfare"  (In Bicycle Motocross (BMX) bicycle racing and mountain biking (MTB), a berm refers to a banked turn made from dirt with a relatively tight radius)

Five colorful birds from the island of Woozoo are sitting on a fence. Each player gets 6 shots per turn to knock them down. When a bird is hit, it flips over and a score is shown. The first player to 250 wins.

State Geography is a deck of cards featuring information about the 50 states of the United States of America.  The game was published by AAA as a travel game.

The game includes rules for 5 different games that can be played with the cards:
  State Memory Match
  Go Travel
  State Rummy
  Wild World Six Draw
  State War
The games are played by either looking at states that have contiguous borders or comparing information on the cards including population, total area and union entrance date. 
 The cards also include facts about each state and a map indicating where the state is in the union.

Die Welt des 31. Jahrhunderts is the complete future-history of the BattleTech world.  Authors Mai and Nick have taken every BattleTech product, including board games, novels, graphic novels, computer games, role-playing products, comic books, and the CCG to create an encyclopedia of every person, place, and event outlined.  This book is profusely illustrated with many color plates and the book is over 500 pages long.  Included are a complete timeline of the BattleTech Universe, complete listings of every BattleTech product in a variety of media, and a list of every BattleMech model in both English and German.  The book sports a fantastic cover by Joern Rauser.

This book was published in German, and although there have been talks of an English translation, no such translation has yet appeared.
Players dodge sharks and other dangers while trying to make their fortune in the South Seas.

boardgame: MSK 1 Extension Kit for 1829 (Northern & Southern Boards)
description:
The 18xx MSK 1 extension kit adds 6 new tiles for either the 1829 Northern Board or 1829 - 2 gray 'universal junction' tiles, and 4 '5-spoke' double-station city tiles that can promote any green station tile, with a half-page of rules for their use.

Expands:

    1829
    1829 Northern Board

boardgame: MSK 2 Extension Kit for 1829 (Southern & Northern boards)
description:
The 18xx MSK 2 Extension Kit for 1829 adds 9 additional trains - 3 x '3T', 2 x '6', 2 x '2+2', 2 x '4+4E' - to 1829 - either 1829 or 1829 Northern Board, with a half-page of rules for their use.

Expands:

    1829
    1829 Northern Board

Contents: 1 playing board, one die, 250 question and answer cards, 25 directional cards, 4 player pawns and 16 scoring rings.

Players must correctly answer questions in each of the four categories when landing on the appropriate square. The player who answers all the categories first must then proceed to the Alumni house and answer one more question. The final question category is to be determined by the other players involved in the game. An exact roll is required before landing on the Alumni House. If the question is answered correctly the game ends.

The object of the game is to beat your opponent's time at a fun task. The fastest times score points. Be the first player to peg to the finish and you're the winner.

Contents of the game include a timer, timer arm, plastic tray, game board, 264 Gotta Second cards, 2 pads of paper and 12 game pegs (2 each of 6 different colors).

Game instructions are printed in English and French.

Be one of Jesus’ disciples as you follow Him around Galilee and Judea.  Learn about miracles, teaching, and famous journeys and where to find them in the Bible.

Beginning at Nazareth, players start out on a journey to other places on the map. As each place is visited an event card is taken from the pile which refers to an event in Jesus’ life which occurred at a certain place. The basic idea is to match up event cards with places visited.

Once you have visited 7 places and you have an event Card for each of those places, you immediately begin your journey to Jerusalem. On arrival you have one minute to memorize the information on your 7 cards. You score one point for each piece of information you remember, with a total of 3 points per card, making a maximum of 21 points. The winner is the one who ends the game with the highest score.

Flex your brainpower to collect matching sets of trees and flowers in the fast-paced fun of Trees and Flowers.  Learn the names of 96 fascinating flowers and trees plus the geographic regions where they grow.

This card game consists of 12 sets of 4 cards each of similar categories of trees and flowers.  The object of each game is to secure as many sets as possible. Skill in playing is acquired by remembering.

Ages 6 and up.

Flex your brainpower to collect matching sets of birds and animals in the fast-paced game of Birds and Animals.  Learn the names of 96 fascinating creatures plus the geographic regions where they live.

This card game consists of 12 sets of 4 cards each of similar categories of birds and animals.  The object of each game is to secure as many sets as possible. Skill in playing is acquired by remembering.

Ages 6 and up.

In Miracles and Pitfalls up to six players race around the board from the Start space to the Finish space, answering Bible questions from one of four different categories (Bible Characters, Places, Objects, and Fill in the Blanks). A player must correctly answer a question before having the opportunity to roll the die to move forward. Most of the spaces on the board correspond to one of the four categories, but some spaces allow a player to choose which category they want to answer a question from. Some spaces require a player to draw a "Miracle" card which will give the player some benefit like skipping ahead on the board, while other spaces require the player to draw a "Pitfall" card. These cards have negative effects like, for example, causing a player to lose a turn. There are spaces that allow players to take shortcuts along the path and other spaces that require a player to take a longer detour. Finally, there are slide spaces which allow a player to slide forward or backward one space to get to a category space that might make it easier for the player to answer the question. The box includes 800 questions, with 4 questions per card.

Ages 5 and up.

How well do you know your Bible?  Do you know which book comes before Habakkuk?  How many chapters are there in the book of Titus?

Bible Bonanza is a package of six Bible based card games that will test your knowledge of Biblical trivia.  The included games are:

1. Bible Pairs
2. Bible Runs
3. Book Piles
4. Book Ends
5. Challenging Chapters
6. Impasse!

Ages 5 and up.

Bible Scramble is a game that turns Bible trivia into lively entertainment. It is both a battle of wits and a race against time as players flip over cards and dig into the letter pile to spell out their answers to Bible trivia.

The object is to be the first person to spell out the answers to your card questions with letter tiles, and to clean your opponents out of shekels.  Each card supplies a Bible text just in case memory fails.

Ages 10 and up.

Card game for 2+ players.
Each player controls a squadron of Privateers. 
The object of the game is to successfully lead your pilots on missions vs pirate gangs, enemy militias, and commercial zeppelins.

VICTORY Conditions
The player with the most VP (Victory Points) at the end of the game wins.
The game ends when a player goes to draw a card and there are no cards left in the deck.
VP are gained by completing missions.

Players share a common deck. That deck contains 6 types of cards: H = Pilots, P = Planes, W = Weapons, M=Maneuvers,
D = Damage, V = Missions

Game also uses two six sided dice 
Counters are used to mark damage to the planes.


Each player is dealt 7 cards. 
Roll high on 1D6 to see who goes first.

On ones turn the player goes through 6 phases;
Luck Phase
Recruit Phase
Mission Phase
Defender Phase
Action Phase
End Phase

Completing misions scores points Whoever at the end of the game has completed the most missions or who has destroyed aircraft who are attempting to complete a mission is the winner.

A Medley of 4 Music Games

Using cards, a spinner, a 3D gameboard and a cassette tape, children play 4 progressively harder games to learn about the orchestra. Pawns depict children playing various instruments, and games consist of activities like a race to see whose orchestra will be the first on stage. The cassette has a story on one side, with side two being sounds of instrument families - you must identify the strings, brasses, woodwinds and percussions. Games are aimed at children Ages 7 - 10

In Colossal Fossil Fight, each of the two players use a dinosaur to pick up bones from the bone yard and place them into their den.  There are three types of bones (legs, ribs, and skulls) which are worth one, three, and five points, respectively.  Once all the bones are removed from the bone yard, the player with the most bone points in their den is the winner.

In this game, players choose to be their favorite Shirt Tales cartoon character and move around the three-dimensional board to collect their four shirt tokens. The first to collect their tokens and get to the top of the large oak tree is the winner.

Playable with any 16 dice (25 in a four-player game) and some pawns, Qurush is a quick abstract game where each player is attempting to rearrange the dice to match a secret pattern they design before the game begins.

Full rules (and downloadable "qubes") are available at the Invisible City Productions website:

http://www.invisible-city.com/play/303/qurush

This game, produced and sold in Mexico, is a clone of Monopoly.  Dice are rolled, the airplane tokens are moved, states of Mexico (properties) are purchased, etc. There are also Mexican Train lines to buy, Lottery and Surprise cards to draw, as well as the famous "collect 200 pesos as you pass Go" space.
It also comes with plastic chips to play checkers with.

Elementeo is an educational card game developed by Anshul Samar.  Chemical elements can be combined to form compounds that can be used in the game.

Homepage:  http://www.elementeo.com

Description from the Maker:

In the Elementeo Chemistry Card Game (v1), elements have their own personalities -- Oxygen becomes Oxygen Life-Giver, Sodium becomes Sodium Dragon, and Iodine becomes Iodine Mermaid. Players can create compounds and play with element in the game. Elementeo includes five different levels (element, reaction, compound, alchemy, and fusion) and has elements, compounds, and alchemy cards (which include black holes and nuclear fusion).  The goal of the game is to capture your opponent's electrons and reduce them to 0.

What People Have Said About The Elementeo Chemistry Card Game (v1):

"This young inventor has stumbled upon something that will stimulate minds and make people more interested in science” - Dr. Tom Lane, Past President, American Chemical Society.

"Elementeo teaches kids to see elements and compounds not as boring things to be memorized but as interesting personalities and powerful agents at work in the world. Which is of course exactly how real chemists and scientists see them. If they come away from the game remembering a fun fact about an element, or understanding the basic notions of bonding and charge, they will have a powerful head start in understanding chemistry" - Theodore Gray, co-founder of Wolfram Research (makers of Mathematica) and Popular Science columnist.

“Elementeo builds on games like Magic: The Gathering and Pokémon by appealing to kids with its fun factor, but also focusing on the education side. The world should take notice.” - Peter Adkison, CEO of Hidden City Games, and former CEO of Wizards of the Coast (the makers of Magic: The Gathering and distributors of Pokémon).

Update: The first version of Elementeo (v1) has sold out, the next version (v2) is expected to hit amazon.com in the beginning of summer 2012. Alchemist Empire, Inc also released an Elementeo App on the Apple App Store for students to explore the elements and create their own Elementeo elements.

Sockeye follows the life-cycle of a Sockeye Salmon.  It is designed to be a fun educational exploration of salmon information.

Players begin as salmon eggs which hatch into fry.  The fry grow into fingerlings in a lake and then proceed to the ocean.  This course is reversed as the salmon returns to the same spawning bed were it was born.  Along the way there are many hazards to the growing salon; from river dangers to being eaten by animals and people.

The game is a standard roll-and-move variety.  The frequent hazard spots move the player back or forward a number of spaces; make a player miss a turn; or require a particular number to be rolled before continuing.  The last hazard, three spaces from the finish, is particularly nefarious as it sends the player all the way back to the beginning.

The game comes with a map of the Sockeye spawning rivers of BC Canada and a detailed description of the life-cycle of a Sockeye Salmon.  The game artwork is beautiful, from the board to the fish-shaped playing pawns.

In Caterpiggily, players take turns drawing duel-colored tiles which represent sections of a caterpillars body, and placing the like colors next to each other thus building the caterpillar.  Player continue around the table placing their tiles until one player has no more tiles to place. This player then places the head and is declared the winner.

User Summary:

A fun game for 4 to 12 year-olds where you match colors from you hand to form a caterpillar. The goal of the game is to be the first to place his or her last card thus having the privilege of placing the head of the caterpillar. Each player starts with 5 cards. Each card has two colors. Each player matches the color at the end of the caterpillar with the color on one of their cards. If they don't have the color they have to draw until they get it. If all of the cards run out and you still don't have the color you have to pass.

boardgame: Age of Steam Expansion: War in Iraq & New York Subway
description:
War in Iraq
This Age of Steam expansion takes place in the desert of Iraq. The war has concluded, and lucrative oil fields have recently been discovered. Your goal is to ship the oil to the US and Europe to enrich your business. The US Army will, of course, aid your cause...

New York Subway
This Age of Steam expansion takes place in New York City, and you have to construct a subway beneath the skyscrapers. This map features innovative rules which drastically augment both the track building and goods shipment phases of the game.

boardgame: Barbed Wire Bullets & Bayonets: Fast Play World War I Rules
description: Fast play WW I skirmish rules.  Ground scale is 1" = ten feet.  Units are generally platoons; larger games involving many platoons may be done.  Under ten pages of rules, plus several pages of charts.  Information for tanks and artillery is included.

Fast play WW II rules scaled to actions from company to task force sized.  General organizational information provided from platoons to battalions.  Written from 15mm-25/28mm scale, but convertible to 5/6 mm if desired.

Less than seven pages of rules, plus some charts with numerous weapons and AFV values differentiated.

In The Wandering Merchant, players play the roles of merchants buying and selling goods. They drive carts pulled by horses transporting them between small villages separated by dangerous wilderness areas in a fantasy setting. The goal is to be the first player to earn a fixed amount of money. Players can also upgrade their carts to transport more goods per trip or buy more horses to travel faster.

This game was an entry to the Good Portsmanship contest in March 2007, and it's based of Merchant of Venus by Richard Hamblen.

boardgame: Six Gun Sound: Blaze of Glory!
description:
Six Gun Sound is THE cowboy game. Why? Because Six Gun Sound puts you into the Wild West of 1875 to 1885. Why did we pick that ten year period? Well it covers the Lincoln County War, the unpleasantness in Tombstone. The Demise of the James/Younger Gang, the gold fever in the Black Hills that led to the Great Sioux War of 76 as well as the founding of Deadwood, lots of Apache trouble, the Nez Perce, the flight of the Cheyenne from Indian Territory...and a bunch of other stuff.

We’re talking Billy the Kid, Pat Garrett, Wyatt Earp, Doc Holiday, Custer’s Last Stand, Geronimo, and Wild Bill Hickok to name a few. This is the time period that made the Wild West wild and burned it into the hearts and minds of every kid growing up. Except in Six Gun Sound we dig deeper into it to let you “live” the time period. How so? You choose what you want to be. Marshal, Outlaw, Ranger, there’s up to six different professions. So different that each one has a unique Encounter system that allows you fight the battles that these Gunfighters, Cowboys, and others would fight. What’s the “Encounter” system? It’s what tells you who, what, and when you’re going to meet when it’s fighting time. Now add this to the “Campaign” system and you’re not just gaming the Wild West, you’re living it!

With the “Campaign” system you can travel all across the US and into Canada or Old Mexico if you like. We’re talking about 22 different areas that you can travel through. Each one is rated by how “civilized” it is…or not. For example, so when you go through Indian Territory expect to find…you guessed it, Indians. All the work of dreaming up your next bank robbery, gunfight, cattle drive and many other encounters are already done. We’ve done all the work, we’ve built and populated your world, and all you have to do is enjoy and play the game!
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
The aim of the game is to get both your harpoons into the shark and tow him to one of the ports.

Play consists of rounds with players moving their boats and putting out the bait followed by the sharks move.

First the shark surfaces and then moves towards a boat where the shark tries to overturn the boat or the boat attempts to harpoon the shark

The game uses both spinners and Dice for movement and determining the outcome of actions.

Gerry Anderson's Space Precinct Crime Busters Board Game!

Each player is issued with a case to solve. By moving around Demeter City in their Police Cruisers players must gather evidence and locate and apprehend their suspect.

Players move by spinner or communication cards. Players move to relevant crime scenes to collect evidence. Once the player has collected four evidence tokens (including the three specific ones for their case they can attempt to apprehend the suspect.

To apprehend the player trades unwanted evidence to look at a suspect card.

Once apprehend then its back to the Station to win the game

The Pirelli Rallye Monte Carlo game represents a rally race with the board representing a Leg that consists of four road sections and three special stages.  The object is to be the fastest over three legs or three runs over the entire track.  In the 1980's the sport was a bit different.  Road sections were over public streets and going too fast or too slow garnered a time penalty. The Special stages were over closed public roads, private roads or trails in rural areas and speed was not restricted. In the game, the Road sections have a set number of dice throws in which a player is to travel the section with his car.  You may roll one or two D6 that have numbers 1,2 or 3 on the faces. Finishing in less rolls garners a 10 second penalty and finishing in too many rolls gets a 5 second penalty. For each special stage, a card is drawn with details of that stage and players must try and choose the best tyres for that stage, with weather conditions being a contributing factor.  Then an Unexpected Weather condition card is drawn.  The tire choice combined with info from the two cards modify the die rolls during the Special stage. The player then chooses to roll the red die that has more pips and two crash faces or the blue die with less pips and only one crash face. Along the Special stages are spaces that result in car damage if a player lands on those spaces. Prior to starting the race each player may place Repair Stations along the Road sections to repair damages incurred along the Specials stages. If a player lands on a Damage space in the first Special stage, that Damage penalty, 10 seconds, will be applied thereafter to each section until the player meets the requirement for entering one of his Repair Stations.  The player who completes the three Legs in the fastest time wins the rally/game.

Basically a roll and move game with a bit of flare within the theme of the sport as it was conducted at the time of the game's production.

This being a game for the Pirelli tire company, there is more information concerning tires in the game materials than is necessary to play the game.  The only tyres available are from Pirelli and the game materials seek to show how each tyre should be used, with the rulebook containing a lot of information about how rallies were organized and run at that time and how to choose tyres for each stage.

Adaptation of the comic strips series published by Soleil, designed by Severine Gauthier, Thomas Labourot and Christian Lerolle, "Extraordinary Adventures: Noodles!" propose you to live the history of Kikitte, Isha, Peko and Mayoke.
 Discover the magic universe of Noodles! by achieving the quest which your Sentetsu Master entrusted to you. Martial arts, special abilities and various weapons, you have in hand the keys to success.

Mix of boardgame, card game and role playing game, Noodles! make it possible to play alone or to gather five players around a great adventure rythmed by all kinds of tests, enigmas, searches and fights.

Difficulty level can be adapted and the course of the play can be modified by choosing different options included in the game rules.

Ready to fight Doragon dragon and the terrible lady of snows? Then on the way for the adventure!

Board game of naval/air warfare with capture of islands/bases.

Game features perhaps one of the largest boxes in the history of game publishing--24 1/4 inches by 18 1/4 inches by one-and-a-half inches.  The hard-mounted board is twice this size.  The single combat chart is the size of the box.

The box top features a reproduction of a hand-drawn charcoal-pencil picture of an aircraft carrier flight deck and island with crewmen on deck, with ships and planes in the background along with yellow-and-red shell bursts, muzzle flashes and black smoke plume.

Rules are contained in a single-folded 8 1/2 inch by 11 inch sheet.

Components include Board, Battle Chart, twenty cards (numbered from One to Ten), two dice and two fleets of miniature vac-u-formed ships in red and blue, plus eight injection-molded miniature 105 mm guns in silver.

Board is a grid of one-inch white-outlined squares on a blue sea.  Along one side of the board is a display for each carrier deck plus a Dry Dock and Shipyard display for each player.

Along each baseline is a Home Base and Airfield.  A sixteen-square island with an Airfield and four fort zones occupies the center of each half of the game board.

Each Fleet consists of two Aircraft Carriers, 3 Battleships, 4 Landing Barges, 5 Planes, 2 Cruisers, 2 Destroyers, and 4 Submarines.

The "Battle Chart" has 26 columns, one for each possible attacker/defender type combination, plus a column for "Units of equal strength."  Ten results are provided for each column including outcomes such as Sunk, Damaged, Return to Shipyard, Retreats, Escapes, and more.

Victory is achieved by either sinking the enemy Fleet or capturing the enemy's Island Base.

Toomey Toys was located in Harrisburg, PA.
This early and simple game was made by a company in Bavaria for English-speaking countries.

Miniature wargame rules for the Dark Ages up through Norman Conquest.  Originally published in "Miniature Wargames" Magazine, No. 7, 1983.  Referred to on occasion as "D.A.I.S" or "The D.A.I.S. System."

Made available through Wyre Forest Games Club as a free download:

The game strives to capture the "feel" of the period using two or three "divisions" or "battles" of grouped warrior bands of Retinues and Contingents, rather than a number of independently-maneuvering "units" of like-armed soldiers.

The qualities of Aggression, Formation and Strength are key along with Leadership which seeks to maximize these qualities to gain advantage over opposing troops.  These qualities ebb and flow depending on the fortunes of the unit, nearby friendly units, and Leaders.

The actual resolution of combat is literally resolved by each player engaging in a single round of rock/paper/scissors for each pair of units, or aligned groups of soldiers from a unit, brought together in melee.

A simple set of aliens-vs-humans skirmish combat rules inspired by films such as "Aliens."  Three pages of rules plus diagram of an installations for initial scenario.

Rules for alien encounters, firing, "mortal combat" (melee), wounds, noncombatants, aliens' ability to use captured weapons.

Miniature wargame rules for naval actions in the American Civil War, intended for 15mm scale figures and miniature ships but also usable with 10mm and, with some record keeping, 1/600 or smaller ship miniatures (with player-provided movement/range conversions).

Rules cover armored vessels, steam and wind-powered ships, submarines, ramming, ship vs. fortification, and boarding actions.  Leadership, crew quality, various weight of guns, damage control, grounding, fires, about 14 pages of rules and a two-sided removable card with game tables.

Fast-play English Civil War miniatures rules, scaled for 15 - 28mm figures with provision for measurement conversion to 5, 6 or 10mm scales.

Seven pages of rules, two pages of charts.  Three types of infantry, three types of cavalry, light and heavy guns differentiated along with five possible troop qualities from Rabble to Arrogant.

Battalion/Regiment scale miniature Renaissance-era wargame, designed for fast play.  Eight pages of rules, two pages of charts.

Scaled for 15-28mm miniatures with provision for 5, 6 or 10mm with measurement conversion.  Four types of infantry, four types of cavalry, light and heavy guns represented.

A boardgame allowing two players to fight out a fictional campaign from the Napoleonic Wars: a French invasion of England's Isle of Wight. Full rules are provided, with period illustrations and fictional vignettes conveying the feel of the game setting. Provided with the cut-outs needed to make the four-part A2 board and 93 playing counters. A game should last 90 minutes on a first play, reducing to an hour once familiar with the rules.

Monopoly themed around the Village of Granville, Ohio to celebrate the Granville Bicentennial. Features many of the historic locations and businesses around the community.

The classic boardgame was inspired by Sam Kapland-Goland, created by Mrs. Jeanna Giovannell's 4th Grade Discovery Class of the Granville Intermediate School.  This version of Monopoly requires players to accumulate wealth by acquiring businesses while avoiding charges for the customers. Community Chest and Chance cards are replaced by Fate and Bicentenial Chest cards.  The In Court and Loading Zones spaces replace the Free Parking and Jail spaces.  A must for Ohio history fans.

Drakons and Gödenors fight for the conquer of space. Due to a  the simultaneous explosion of several stars, the universe collides in only one dimension.

Drakons are oviparous and enormous. They gather in developed communities, with high technology. Their strength and aggressiveness is remarkable.

Gödenors haven't a special natural strength but a high level of intelligence, becoming one of the most dangerous races in the world.

Both races have met, unfortunately, and so have created two forces that will fight to the death for the control of the new world, to conquer a new place to live and improve.

Drakon's force is commanded by Clain, the Manan, and is composed by two Dux-Manan and Atter-Manan, Arnor and Cowtor which have in charge three expert Mereos: Sunno, Cornex and Artak.  Gödenors have the same forces in their side: Arco(Manan), Daro(Dux-Manan), Rotx(Atter-Manan) and the Mereos(Sazzer, Preto y Malk).

The goal of the game is to conquer the opposite military base. It's achieved by getting one the characters to the mountainous enemy base.

Each of the characters have different attributes that can be used in combat against enemy creatures.

Each player starts with only the Manan and will get through the different places by throwing a dice. Your forces could be increased by gathering new soldiers and getting additional powers, all of these using cards, supplied in Oracle squares. Other special squares will grant you access to mountains (Climbing) or get you frozen (Dimensional Door).

To stop enemy forces in their advance to your base, you can attack them with your ground forces, by placing them adjacent or by using the heavy artillery placed in mountains. Artillery will give you the opportunity to fire one of the missiles, trying to knock down opposing forces.

There are additional powers attained through extra cards.

From the Instructions:

"Test your memory with Shrek and all his twisted fairytale friends!  Just turn over the cups to find half a fairytale character, then see if you can find the other half!"
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Puss in Boots

description:
Super Force Seven (SF7) is a Tactical RPG / Miniatures Adventure Wargame allowing for battles between super powered characters of all kinds. The game can be played as a typical tactical miniatures game, but really shines when the options for added depth are used, allowing it to be played more like a light-RPG or a campaign/story driven tactical miniatures game.

Game play is fast paced and simple, and the well thought out design system allows you to easily build your favorite characters from comic books, television, the movies or from your own imagination and lets you pit these characters against each other in a variety of scenarios. These scenarios can range from a simple brawl in the streets to a battle of wits between two masterminds, or anything else you can think of, making this much more than just another game of superhero combat.

As of November 2008, no further information on this game is available, and the homepage is no longer registered.  This game may be pure vapor.

From the publisher:

A fantastic and dangerous travel game, a lot of fun for the whole family.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
This wargame is a recreation of the Greek Revolution during 1821-1829 using miniatures.

The game contains:
-42 page booklet (in Greek) with rules and historic information, six starting scenarios.
-the basic miniatures which are 54 in total, 27 miniatures representing the Greek forces and 27 miniatures representing the Turkish forces. More miniatures can be bought separately.
-terrain models (rocks, trees, houses etc)
-ruler and dices.

Designed by Spyros Koumousis and published by Aiolos press in 2001. There is only a Greek edition available.

Kiddi Golf is a 9 hole tabletop miniature golf course in a box. 9 Wooden holes, each featuring different obstacles normally made out of metal. Game has small wooden clubs which are used to try and play each of those holes in as few shots as possible.
Each player starts with one of four different game boards and all the puzzle pieces in the middle.  Players spin on a color wheel and chose a matching colored puzzle piece that fits their board.

XAGA- Das Stadtspiel is a creative boardgame. The game begins with a city that wants more color from ideas and strategies. Variety is desired, because instead of quantity the citizens are concerned with the quality of the city. On the city-plan, the players alter buildings, plants and so on, and attempt to bring in inhabitants and guests. However the building alone are not sufficient. A clever and imaginative advertisement is fundamental to winning the game.

In the constant role-change between investor and inhabitant the players together create their own place of fantasy and strategy. Communication is very important! The end the election for the "Citizen of Honor" decides the win. To win the title, one does not only need the votes, but also the "most buildings", the "most beautiful house", the "most disturbing neighbor" and several other categories.
publishers: Netzwerk-Arbeitsgemeinschaft zur Förderung einer gemeinwesen-orientierten Sozialstruktur Leipzig Süd-Ost e.V.

description:
A downloadable game portraying the Mexican assault on the Alamo.  The Alamo Compound and six immediate approaches are depicted on a map that can be adequately printed on a single 8/5 x 11-inch sheet.

Units are activated on the basis of Command Rolls.  Twenty-nine Mexican units (roughly companies) face ten Texan personnel units and eight artillery markers.

The Alamo complex is represented by walls and buildings of varying strength which can be reduced as a result of attacks.  Defenders may be outside or inside certain structures.  Mexican formations have stacking limits and may spread from their original appearance area by oblique movement.

The rules amount to approximately seven pages.  Optional rules, examples of play and designer's notes are also included.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description: This is the first NFL authorized Monopoly edition, and also the first Super Bowl Monopoly game. Comes with 8 football-themed computer tokens.

This British WWI era game features the cartoon characters Pip, Squeak & Wilfred who appeared in the Daily Mirror.  The board references WWI and players are encouraged to read the Daily Mirror daily to catch the latest adventures of these characters.
Ahead of its time, this game features a modular board.  The players create a track and then race their cars around it.

The object of the game is to be the first player to fly their four UFO's back from outer space and land them on Planet Earth.

On your turn you cover your eyes with the mask provided while another player moves the large UFO round the board. As it moves it "beeps" and you need to identify where it landed. If you guess right then you can move your UFOs and "ZAP" another player back to outer space. If you get it wrong then the other players get a free move.

Not quite as easy as it sounds (especially if your hearing isn't that good) and you can also "freeze other ufo's by landing on them.

This game is intended for ages three and up.

The eleven game boards are stacked and the princess pawn is placed at the start of the top board. The players then take turns moving the princess until she reaches the hole in the board, causing the active player to earn a token and allowing the top board to be removed and movement to continue on the next board. The game ends when the end of the last board is reached and the players count their tokens to see who has the most.

This game is basically a vehicle for memorizing Scripture to overcome sin and temptation.  Players use the spinner to move around the board.  After landing on a "Wisdom Card" space, a player has the opportunity to collect 1 to 3 Wisdom cards if he can memorize "Commands of Christ" (Bible verses) printed on the cards.  A Temptation space causes a player to go to one of the 3 pits, where he must memorize a verse for overcoming temptation.  If the verse can't be quoted within the allotted time frame, a Pit card must be drawn and acted upon with negative results such as making the player lose cards already collected.  The player with the most cards collected at the end of the game is the winner.

In Cinemania you are a movie producer who competes against the other players to release 5 movies of several different genres (Science Fiction, Comedy, Horror, Adventure, and so on), trying to get the best Box Office for each release.
Each movie needs several requisites to be produced, and these are met by the correct playing of various kind of cards (Actor/Actresses, Director, Effect -like Cinematography, Soundtrack, Special Effects, so on - and special cards). You may also play bonus and malus cards to favour your movies and damage the competing productions.
There are also Event cards and the race to the Oscars. Winner is determined over various scores (Best movie in each genre, Oscars won, Total Income, Objective cards, and so on)

The game contains:

    261 full color cards for the various decks needed for play
    1 individual playing board for each player
    Other playing aids


To get the printed DTP version you may ask to pumaye@virgilio.it

Print and play versions are available at wargamedownloads.com and arima.it.

Take a dive for sunken treasure and then try to escape the clutches of ORVILLE THE OCTOPUS.  The game was made by the  Eberhard Faber Company in 1962. Orville is an 8 inch wide metal Octopus with flasher eyes. He balances on a center post in his seascape background. Danny Diver a metallic deep sea diver with a magnet embedded in his feet is suspended on a rubber string connected to a rubber ball. Treasure pieces in the form of gold balls, coins and anchors are scattered on the sea floor. The object is to pick up as much plunder as you can but if you get too close to Orville he spins around and, with a sickening metallic ping sound,  he grabs Danny.

Experience all the thrills and excitement of Major League Baseball.  You are the manager of a National or American League team vying for first place and on to the World Series.  Everyone can play.  Your six year old (by the roll of dice) plays right alongside Mom and Dad while they answer the Trivia question cards.   There are over 500 True and False, multiple choice or direct response questions on 100 cards.  All this happens on two different parts of the unique gameboard with Manager and Umpire cards getting into the act adding to the frustration and momentum that are all part of the real baseball world.   This is an officially licensed product of Major League Baseball.

A simple roll and move game in which players collect cards to overcome obstacles in their bicycle ride.   Players roll the dice and advance their pawns on the gameboard. If player landed on certain squares with lamp, oilcan, et c., he takes the card with the item and may use it during the game, e.g., if he landed on squares 20/30 and he doesn't have lamp or bell he must go back to squres 5/18 and get one. There are 9 type of cards, 3 cards each.

Cyclist who reaches first the square with 100 wins the race.

Das realistische Futballspiel von Premiere World

This game features the teams of the German Bundesliga for the season 2001/2002.
A card game Ages 5 - 12. The first player to build his bug-house wins the game. Game comes with cards, plus enough plastic lots, Chimneys, Hammers, Saws, Trowels, and cardboard walls for 4 players.

boardgame: Sternkrieg: A Game of Interstellar War
description:
Sternkrieg is a game of interstellar exploration and conquest. Each player represents a colony in the same 'constellation' (per the rules) which builds ships and sends them out to explore other regions, gaining raw materials which can be used to build more ships. Players can also use their ships to attack each other. Combat takes place either on a tactical hex grid (provided by the players, as would be the counters) or on a table using miniatures.

Ships and fortresses (which protect planets) are customizable, being built using a point system.

The game was first published in 1976, with a second edition released in 1977. The differences between the two are not known.

boardgame: Starship: The Game of Space Contact
description:
Starship is a fairly elaborate tactical simulation of a contact between two starships, each of which has an mission that is unknown to the other ship.

One player's ship is designated as the "intruder" and the other is the "intercepter" (sic). At the beginning of the game, each player secretly chooses a mission. The possible missions vary depending on the player's role, intruder or intercepter.

The missions include reconnaissance, space exploration, piracy, raid, and patrol (under either a "cold war" status or active hostilities). Each mission provides conditions for a "strategic victory" (such as capturing the other vessel, making peaceful contact, etc.) and orders which, if violated, will prevent the player from gaining a "command victory" (e.g., a player might be ordered to not fire first). Victory points are given for strategic, command, and tactical victories (the latter being based on ship damage), and these are compared to see who won (if anyone, as a possible outcome is that neither player wins).

The game takes place in three-dimensional space, but the hex map is only used to show the relative positions of the ships. Markers placed on the map show a ship's vertical distance above or below the plane. A small map can be used because at the start of each turn the map is effectively rotated to put the Z-axis at the center along the line between the ships.

Players write down and execute their movement and firing orders simultaneously. The main concern in battle is preventing one's systems from overheating, which can occur due to acceleration/deceleration as well as combat.

While the game's envelope gives the publication year as 1976, all the components say 1975. It seems possible the envelope was added to the game after the first copies were produced.

Starship & Empire is a strategic space conflict game with a tactical combat resolution system.

The strategic map consists of 1-4 sectors, each of which is a 10x10 grid with each space having 10 height indicators, thus making each sector a 10x10x10 cube. Star systems are located in various spots on these maps and serve various functions depending on the scenario chosen.

Players write down movement orders for their fleets and execute them simultaneously. When fleets encounter each other, combat is resolved on a large hex map, with players again writing orders, which now include weapons fire as well as movement.

Ships are rated for energy (usage of which must be tracked), rest mass, crew and computer ability, and cargo capacity.

A number of different scenarios are provided, some of which require conquering the other player's systems and some of which have other objectives, such as a pirate vs. merchant scenario.

boardgame: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp
description: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp, the new Western game of adventure for 2 to 4 players is based on the career of the historic U.S. Marshal (as seen on the popular TV program).  It's a game of strategy and luck which youngsters Ages 7 - 12 will find absorbing get-together fun.  Pursuing outlaws and bandits, capturing them on a "fast draw" and bringing them prison offers the exciting competitive play recommended by child psychologists.

NoGlobal is for 2-5 players, and each player takes on a different role—President, policeman, demonstrator, or violent protester—and each role has a different win condition: the President wants to visit three monuments in town, the violent protester has to throw a tart at the President, and so on.

NoGlobal is available for download through Arima.it.

(information from www.boardgamenews.com)

boardgame: Doodlebops Get on the Bus! Game
description: Based on the children's TV show/performers, this game is a basic roll and move.  The goal is to get the Doodlebops characters on the bus in time for their concert. Players try to collect all three Doodlebops instruments - keytar, guitar and drum set along the way. The first player with all the instruments and all the Doodlebops on the bus is a winner.  Includes: Game Board, 12 Doodlebops Play Pieces, 12 Instrument Chips, Die and Instructions. No reading is required, Ages 4 and up.

The Mexican Rails kit was originally conceived as an expansion to Empire Builder and available directly from Mayfair. It includes some new cards, tokens, and a map of Mexico that could be aligned with the Empire Builder board, but all the components were black & white copies packed in a 9×12 envelope with a Mexican Rails sticker on it. Rather than upgrade to production quality similar to the base game, the board, loads, and cards were incorporated into North American Rails.

It was also published in Games International (Issue 7 – July 1989) magazine.

Expands:

    Empire Builder

Let's Go To McDonald's Game is a child's game. Players race along a colorful track to be the first to reach McDonald's. The game teaches color recognition and matching, turn taking, and fair play. Each player controls a "Happy Meal Toy" character (Grimace, Birdie the Early Bird, The Hamburgler, and of course, Ronald McDonald himself) and advance along the path avoiding obstacles such as a duck crossing, an open manhole, a skunk, and a broken fire hydrant. Randomly placed tiles along the way will either send you forward or backwards on the path because as the game itself states, "Anything can happen on the way to McDonald's!" The first player to reach the Golden Arches is the winner.

A giant, jungle themed, roll and move game for 2 - 4 players Ages 3 and up. Measures 28" X 38" and is meant to be played on the floor.

This is not the same game as Knizia's King of the Beasts: Mythological Edition.

From the back of the box:

The American quest for fame and fortune. Four different levels of fun to choose from. A real American Made Game. Find security, peace of mind, wealth. Set goals and plan strategies.

An educational board game made to illustrate the destructive nature of addiction.  Its mechanics are described by the author as a reverse of games like Monopoly; instead of acquiring wealth, the players lose money, their job, and their possessions throughout the course of play.

From the author's own description:

"Up to [seven] players play at being heroin addicts... the last to lose all his assets is the winner... It was meant as a kind of teaching aid for people who worked for drug programs, or wanted to learn that sort of thing.

"Three to five [players] is ideal...  You roll dice and advance to heavier habits ($10/day, $20/day, etc.)
as you spiral inward.  There are Hassle and Wisdom cards, when you land on red or green squares, kind of like Chance and Community Chest in Monopoly."

When the player loses his or her job due to varying circumstances, hustles help replace player income.  These could be described as variable player powers, because in a sense, different hustles earn a player income in different ways.

As the game progresses, players are likely to acquire more expensive habits which require more money to maintain, as well as suffering the loss of their job, arrests by law enforcement, and negative conseqences of board spaces.  The player who survives the longest is declared the winner, though the true goal of the game is to show that getting involved in the real-life junkie game is always a losing proposition.
Pizza Pests-A Pizza-building Card Game for Pizza Lovers. Memory-Matching Game where cards are designed like pizza slices.

The "Fun-Full" Thrift Game is designed to teach good saving principles. It consists of fifteen pairs of cards and one odd card. The cards display text that promotes the value of saving money.
The cards are all dealt to the players. Play consists of drawing a card from the player on the right to match cards in the acting players hands. The player with the odd card left at the end of the game is the loser.

From Games et al and used with permission:
This is a truly fun game manufactured by C W Faulkner & Co in 1899 and is a race game covering the, then, popular hobby of cycling. Falulkner produced a series of games similar to Bikee called the ‘Upidee Series’ Cards are laid out to provide the course and the use of a teetotum provides the means of movement. This game consists of up to six cyclists, two dice, the rules and 20 illustrated course cards. The bottom of the box lists other games by Faulkner.

Note: Rules stipulate teetotum but d6 die is pictured with game in BGG gallery. Another ebay sale has images showing a four-sided teetotum with sides numbered 1-4.

Coloured balls are drawn at random from the bag and placed onto the board, filling up all the spaces.

Each player then draws one coloured figure at random to determine which coloured balls they will control and places it in one of the four home areas.

Some neutral coloured balls are then removed depending on the number of players before the game begins.

Players take turns moving either one of their own balls or a neutral ball and the game ends when one player gets all of their own coloured balls into their home area.

An Exciting New Board Game that Incorporates Military Strategy and Economic Manipulation

Every territory generates one of 7 products. Every round of 7 turns you collect cash for what you own. This cash is used to build armies, navies and missiles, as well as finance moves and wars.

Start out as small tribes, scattered all over the world. Your resources are limited. Create armies and expand into city-states, grabbing high-value product. Convert this product into war materials.

As you conquer more territories you gain more resources. Claim countries and get bonuses. Build up your defenses. Protect your sea lanes with navies. Send out marines to claim new territories and islands.

As you grow, so do your opponents. Clashes are inevitable. Everyone wants the high-value product. Wars break out. Missiles fly.

When you reach super-power status, with an inventory of nuclear and conventional missiles, natural barriers are no longer a problem. You have the resources to launch large-scale attacks.

There is no randomness to a battle. As in chess, the attacker always wins. But your opponent may see a gap in your defenses and turn the tables on you. You could win a battle but lose the war.

Should you build your defenses? Or attack your opponent when he's weak? Remember, he who strikes first has the advantage.

Grow your empire and dominate the world!

Home Page: http://www.ajcraft.net
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
"Dora, David and Dorothy are donkeys: but not any old kind of donkey, special donkeys.  They are really little golden donkeys.  With a bit of luck they are happy to give away some of their precious treasures.  Therefore you have to have some luck and uncover the right cards..."

Players reveal tiles that instruct them to collect one of three colored donkeys.  In subsequent turns, the donkeys will move from owner to owner until the "we give you coins" tile is turned up.  At this point, players collect coins for any donkeys that are in front of them.  The game also comes with tiles to use with "elder players."  Even with the additional tiles, the game is purely chance.  But children will need to interpret the new directions (ex: all donkeys move to the left).

Children learn colors, taking turns, counting to three, and simple directions (left/right).

This is a party game focused on the mechanic of team's guessing humorous rhymed pairs of words.  In alternating turns, each team's clue giver selects a card and begins describing the two word rhyme using words and/or pantomime (like in charades).  When the correct rhyme is guessed, the clue giver draws another card, with the goal being to guess as many rhymes in 60 seconds.  Examples of rhyming pairs of words include:  "Pooch Smooch," "Moose Juice," "Jet Sweat" and so on.

boardgame: See It and Sign It American Sign Language game
description:
From the publisher:

The SEE IT and SIGN IT Game by Bill & Bud, Inc. is an easy-to-follow game that teaches ASL, (American Sign Language,) the fun way! It's 6 games in one, plus a DVD with live action demonstrations. Each of the included 60 word cards shows how to sign a different word, and the Secret Decoder Lenses reveals the hidden word on the back of each card.  The game includes 10 two-sided game boards that allow players to with either just 30 words first or all 60 words.  Six different games can be played with the cards and game boards. Winner of Dr. Toy Award for ten best socially responsible products.

This is a game aimed pre-teens and teens using the theme of "High School Musical"  Players compete to make it through East High School(represented on the game board) to be the first to collect six different cast cards while trying to avoid detention.  Players succeed by answering trivia questions or singing/dancing along with an included interactive music CD.

Brilliantly designed game presents 3 ways to play. Traditional dominoes, build your own dominoes where players create their tiles each turn, and a 2 dimensional version where players can build up as they go. Includes 56 squares and 16 connector joints.

With this expansion you can play Ca$h'n Gun$ with 9 players now. 
Also there are new weapons (Tantos, Shuriken & Shotgun), new cards with special powers and some nice object-cards.

Expands:

    Ca$h 'n Gun$

Top-Gun is a game about Jet Air-Combat between NATO/Warsaw Pact Fighters. Game contains a sheet of counters that have to be cut out, a map with squares (instead of hexes) and several data sheets about specific aircraft. Also included are about 10 small pages of rules and 3 dice.

A horse riding game for up to six players based on 3 Day eventing, Dressage & Cross Country.

The Dressage phase of the game is played with 2 dice - one denoting how many squares the competitor should move & the other the results of that move.

The Dressage & Cross Country phases in Double Event can be played as independent games or the scores combined for a Double Event.

From the creators of International Jump Off.

Tenacety is an extremely entertaining card game suitable for the whole family. It is played on a peg board & utilizes only the 10's, Jacks, Queens, Kings & Aces of a pack of cards - hence the name Ten-ace-ty !

The game ends when one player has managed to remove all 5 of his pegs from the pegboard having won tricks containing those cards he had originally pegged.

Great family card game, simple to learn & play !

Contents

1 x Playing board
20 x Playing cards (10's, Jacks, Queens, Kings & Aces)
20 x Pegs in 4 colours
Rules on reverse of box

Board consists of a trail with three separate parts. A central part, where the players must combine 5 balls in two colors to match the pattern they have at hand, and a left and right section with three balls each, where the players put the balls to feed the central section.

Very similar (if not identical) to Aya

Calculus is a strategic connection game between black and white. Players alternate pushing stones of their colour onto the board. There is no grid - stones may be pushed to anywhere on the board. When touching stones enclose an area the majority of stones on the perimeter of that area decide who controls the area. It is illegal to create an area which has the same number of black and white stones on its perimeter. Stones that are part of the perimeter of an area are immobilized, but the rest may be pushed again instead of introducing a new stone. The winning player is one who connects their edges of the board.

There is no standard shape of board and no standard stone. You can use a tree stump and real stones if you wish. I prefer to use circular stones.

Calculus may be reproduced for non-commercial purposes.

Power Point files with the rules and a sample game are posted in the files section of this page. (An additional rule up for discussion is posted in the forum: "Arguements about touching".)
family: Organizations: The Game Artisans of Canada

description:
This is an educational Ages 8 and up game designed to teach business concepts like: global markets, return on investment, fixed-cost ratios, etc. as well as teaching skills like math, geography, negotiation, trading and communication.  
 Each player assumes the role of Will Trader, an enterprising duck who aspires to build his Cracker Mills all over the world. Players compete by developing businesses, making investments, and other similar actions while circling the gameboard.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Pyramid Scheme: Legend of the Pharaohs
description: This is an abstract strategy game which utilizes a theme of ancient Egypt.  Pyramid Scheme: Legend of the Pharaohs  can be played by 2 or 3 alternating players or teams of players. Each begins the game with a Base Pyramid comprised of 9 pieces (8 Soldiers and 1 Guardian). Each player's objective is to capture the playing pieces and ultimately, raid and capture the Treasure Chamber within the Base Pyramid of the opponents.

From the publisher:

Xemorpheum (pronounced Zemorpheum) was specifically designed to teach Third World business concepts to students and business people.  The game models a Third World Business environment in an ancient coliseum setting in a far off country called Richmania."

Xemorpheum is played by two to seven players who compete against each other by rolling dice, moving around a coliseum track, drawing cards and paying crossing fees to other players or to the treasury.  Players increase their chance of winning by amassing cash, building factories and training arenas, and assembling armies. To win, one player must avoid sudden death bankruptcy and gather more wealth and power than other players while surviving the myriad of hazards, traps and pitfalls inherent in the game's unique playing environment."

This is a roll and move type trivia game with the added twist of pursuing, fighting and capturing villains.  The game is based on the comic book character "Batman" by DC comics.  It includes 1000 questions covering 65 years and multiple decades of Batman history.

Depending on the space the player lands on, when the player answers a question correctly they receive a power card that gives them bonuses to being either a) Batman or b) a villain.  If the player answers a question correctly from a villain space, they receive a villain card.  The player must then make his way to the currently upturned location card.  Once there, the player attempts to "capture" the villain by using power cards against the player sitting next to them with the added luck of a dice roll for each.  When a player captures 3 villains, he wins the game.

The question cards each include 5 questions representing 5 difficulty levels. i.e.:

1. Batman's secret identity is what playboy billionaire?
2. True or false.  The Wayne manor grandfather clock is set to the time of death of Bruce Wayne's parents.
3. After the Grasshopper stole the batmobile, batman installed an alarm to ensure it never happened again.  What villain was subsequently tripped up by that alarm?
4. Selina Kyle's brother Karl is better known as whom?
5. Several incarnations of the batmobile were designed by what stuntman?

As you can see, the questions get progressively harder.

This is a roll-and-move style trivia game based on the city of Omaha, Nebraska (USA).  The trivia questions are based on local features of the city of Omaha and surrounding area, and are in 5 categories: Sports, History, Entertainment, Arts, and General Trivia.  The game includes 4 Movers, 10-Second Timer, 4 Score Cards, 1 Die, game board, and 320 Question Cards with 1,600 questions.

Loosely themed on the Walt Disney Movie "102 Dalmations," this is a children's game where the players compete to be the first to get rid of their "spots" by placing them on Oddball the dog.  Rolling the oversized plastic dice tells how many spots to affix dog-side. If you roll Cruella on the dice, however, then Oddball or another player gives up some spots to you.  Ages 4 and up.

A simple betting game, based on the roll of three standard 6-sided dice held within a turnable cage.

From the printed rule sheet:
"You may invite any number of people to play. The first step is to set out your equipment and select a person to be Banker. The Banker pays off all bets for any agreed number of plays or time limit at the completion of which another player acts as Banker.
All numbers on the layout do not have to be sold. For example, only two or three people may participate in a single play. The profit percentage is exactly the same as if all numbers were covered. 
When all bets are down on the layout, the Banker gives the cage a turn which raises and drops the dice at the opposite end of the cage. The three numbers on the top surface of the dice are the winners. 
If a bettor's number appears on one die he receives the return of his stake plus a like amount (even money). If it appears on 2 dice he is rewarded with the return of his bet plus twice the amount bet (2 to 1). Three like numbers rewards the players with thrice the amount bet plus the bet (3 to 1).
Lowe's CHUCK-A-LUCK is an exact duplicate of the regulation casino game. It has all the play features of the larger size game and will give players the same high degree of enjoyment."

Subtitled: A Race round Britain.  Amusing, Thrilling, and Instructive
This is a very simple race game designed to help people learn the geography of coastal England, Scotland, and Ireland.  Think of it as Snakes and Ladders in a circle.  Players roll, move, and could hit an obstacle or movement bonus depending on their move.

boardgame: Heroes of Asfar: The Finieous Fingers Expansion Set
description:
This is the third expansion for Heroes of Asfar, the entertainingly chaotic card game in which you try to subdue an opponent's character cards, thereby scoring enough points to win.  This final expansion set of HOA contains 8 legendary Character Cards, and 12 additional Play Deck Cards, including Finieous and many of his friends (from early issues of Dragon Magazine), and items such as Bored-Flak's Wand of Fireballs and the Magic Slingshot Stone.

Expands


    Heroes of Asfar



Microbadges:



    
         - Finieous

         - Mergatroid

    
    
         - Fred

         - Bored Flak

    
    
         - Charley

         - Grond

    
    
         - Ranger Rupert

         - Evil Wizard Kask

boardgame: Heroes of Asfar: The Bag of Chaos
description: This is the second expansion for Heroes of Asfar, an entertainingly chaotic game in which you try to subdue an opponent's character cards, thereby scoring enough points to win.  This expansion contains 4 new character cards, 35 additional play deck cards, including such cards as the Flying Iron Cestus, the Awesome Ring of Wishes, and the Bag of Chaos.

boardgame: Bullets & Ballots: A Learning Game on Central America
description:
This game is designed to give its players first-hand experience with the problems confronting the countries of Central America and with difficult U.S. policy choices in the region. Between 20 and 40 players (this is obviously a classroom game) participate in a simulation of real-world political struggles and conflicts that will raise fundamental issues about Central America and the role of the US in the region.

The game consists of four 15-minute periods of activity followed by a debriefing and policy discussion. Players are divided into five teams: the Guatemalan Government, the Army/Wealthy Class, the Guerrillas, the Peasants/Workers, and the US Government. An initial 15-minute Strategy Session within each team is followed by three 15-minute months of an electoral campaign involving discussions and negotiations among the teams.

Each team presents a brief Position Statement to start the action. Over the course of the campaign, each team may use means ranging from moral persuasion to military force in pursuit of its goals. The Army/Wealthy Class and the Guerrillas may opt to launch offensives. The Army can take over at any time, and they also have the option to "silence" members of any of the other Guatemalan teams. At the end, the team in power has the option of holding an election. All Guatemalans except silenced members and guerrillas are eligible to vote. Peasants/Workers have two votes; others have one. Guerrillas may vote by joining the Peasants.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
publishers: Roosevelt Center for American Policy Studies

description:
This is another game in the Haba Mini-Spiel series for children.  Players start with one monkey, one coconut, and one banana playing piece.  On a turn, the player rolls a die to determine what kind of piece the player will try to take from one opponent.  In order to steal the piece, however, the player must guess which hand their opponent has hidden the piece.  The first player to receive three of any playing piece wins.

The game teaches shape recognition and counting to three.

spielaffe
spielaffe

boardgame: Age of Steam Expansion: Jamaica / Puerto Rico
description:
This new set of expansion maps is fully compatible with Age of Steam and Steam.

Jamaica is a two player map that takes place on the small Caribbean island of the same name
Puerto Rico is a single player map with varying levels of difficulty that can be played in 30 minutes or less.

Original edition maps are not mounted.  They are printed on card stock and cut into 2 pieces.
Age of Steam Deluxe versions are mounted boards.

boardgame: Age of Steam Expansion: Barbados / St. Lucia
description:
These Age of Steam Expansions are compatible with Winsome's original Age of Steam and Steam. Both maps are tight and highly strategic, with Barbados as a single player map with great replayability, and St. Lucia as a two player map with fast playing time and unique features.

Maps are not mounted.  They are printed on card stock and cut into 2 pieces.

boardgame: Age of Steam Expansion: America / Europe
description:
